title: pilosella budensis
intro: Pilosella budensis, also known as Alpine Buttercup, is a perennial plant species in the sunflower family, native to the high-altitude mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. Traditionally, Pilosella budensis has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are derived from its sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The health benefits of Pilosella budensis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Pilosella budensis is often prepared as a tincture or infused in tea, and is used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, such as skin irritations and infections.
